destinations.evidence: The nodebuilder pool-aware EVM construction requires TransactionPoolExt, constructs a BlockVerifier from the supplied pool/provider/EVM config, and attaches it to the executor provider alongside the SPC verifier.

equivalence_argument: The v2.2.0 port delegates node executor assembly to ritual-reth-nodebuilder-internal; this dependency owns pool-backed executor construction and passes the BlockVerifier into the provider exactly where block validation will later invoke it.
notes: Mapped in nodebuilder, the audited dependency that owns Ritual executor construction.
rationale: The primary merge changes build_evm_with_pool to require TransactionPoolExt, create a BlockVerifier from the supplied pool, and call new_with_verification with both SPC and block verification components. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When RitualExecutorBuilder is built with a transaction pool, it must construct a BlockVerifier from that pool and pass it, together with the SPC verifier, into the block executor provider.
suggested_falsification_tests: Start or unit-build a Ritual node path that invokes build_evm_with_pool with a mock TransactionPoolExt, then assert the returned executor provider contains and uses a BlockVerifier wired to that pool during block validation.
summary: Pool-backed nodebuilder construction attaches a pool-backed BlockVerifier to the executor provider.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build through build_evm_with_pool with a mock TransactionPoolExt and assert the returned provider contains a BlockVerifier wired to that pool.
title: Pool-backed executor construction attaches block verification
